title: Determination of Enzyme Activity
author: Tjusls China
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6gthbwn
materials: Infinite M1000 Pro Automatic Microplate Reader

steps.title: Clean 96-Well Plates
steps.instruction: Soak the 96-well plates in 75% ethanol and put the container in ultrasonic cleaner for 30min to 1 hour, then use ddH 2 O to wash these plates several times. Put these clean plates in drying oven at 55°C.

steps.title: Prepare Enzyme Dilutions
steps.instruction: Dilute the enzyme using its buffer. There we pipet 1 μL protein stock solution in 1mL buffer and mix gently. Then pipet 100 μL protein solution and mix with 400μL buffer each time, in order to dilute it as a 5-time gradient.

steps.title: Set Up Reactions in Plate
steps.instruction: Pipet 94μL protein solution into 9 wells in plate, usually choosing B2-D4 area, to set 3 parallel controls. Pipet 94 μL buffer without protein into 3 wells as negative controls. Then add 6μL fluorescent substrate into wells.

steps.title: Configure Microplate Reader Program
steps.instruction: Set up the program in Infinite M1000 Pro Automatic Microplate Reader. Shake for 10 sec at 654 rpm. Kinetic Cycle (to read fluorescent intensity each cycle) Fluorescent measure, 75 cycle, 10sec for each cycle.

steps.title: Start Microplate Reading
steps.instruction: Put the plate in Microplate reader, and click Start button.

steps.title: Analyze Initial Data for Linearity
steps.instruction: When the facility ends testing, save data and import it into GraphPad Prism Software. Use “nonlinear fit” – “straight line” and compare R 2 of lines under different concentrations to pick up the best linear fit one, whose R 2 is most close to 1 .

steps.title: Establish Standard Concentration Gradient
steps.instruction: Take this concentration as standard value, then set up parallel gradient of its 2x, 0.5x, 0.25x, etc. Repeat step 3-5.

steps.title: Calculate EC80 Value
steps.instruction: Calculate the ratio of emission(rE = Q 0 /Q m , Q 0 means the maximum fluorescent intensity of each reaction under different protein concentrations, Q m means the maximum fluorescent intensity of all reactions under different protein concentrations). Use GraphPad Prism Software to calculate EC80 value. Set log(concentration of protein) as X, the rate of emissionas Y. Use “nonlinear fit” – “log(agonist) vs. response—Find ECanything”, input 80 as the value of F parameter.

steps.title: Determine Suitable Protein Concentration
steps.instruction: Usually we use the EC80 value as suitable protein concentration, and it can be adjusted according to the actual situation.

steps.title: Design Experimental Groups
steps.instruction: Design experimental groups with the “N+(N-1) principle”. Since we use PBS as our protein buffer, and class B beta-lactamases are depend on Zn 2+ , so we choose the concentration of NaCl, the concentration of ZnCl 2 , and pH, as variables.

steps.title: Analyze Initial Velocities
steps.instruction: When the facility ends testing, save data and import it into GraphPad Prism Software. Use “nonlinear fit” – “straight line” to calculate the initial velocity of each reaction a.k.a. its slope value. Choose the condition with higher initial velocity.

steps.title: Prepare Final Protein Dilution
steps.instruction: Dilute protein again with the ensured most suitable solution into proper concentration.

steps.title: Prepare Substrate Dilutions
steps.instruction: Dilute the fluorescent substrate as 2-time gradient for 8 groups.

steps.title: Calculate Reaction Initial Velocities
steps.instruction: When the facility ends testing, save data and import it into GraphPad Prism Software. Use “nonlinear fit” – “straight line” to calculate the initial velocity of each reaction a.k.a. its slope value.

steps.title: Perform Michaelis-Menten Fit
steps.instruction: Use “nonlinear fit” – “Michaelis-Menten” to fit Michaelis plot of this beta-lactamase. At the same time the software will calculate kinetic constants Km, Vmax automatically.

steps.title: Calibrate Fluorescence
steps.instruction: Dilute protein as 2-time gradient for several groups. Repeat step 3-5 to measure. Take FI as Y, [S] as X, then use “nonlinear fit” – “straight line” to calculate fluorescent calibration value.
